annotations.label: Fibonacci sequence

anchor.snippet: the Fibonacci sequence is a sequence in which each element is the sum of the two elements that precede it

annotations.note: `Nat.fib` defines the Fibonacci stream with `fib 0 = 0`, `fib 1 = 1`, and `fib (n+2) = fib n + fib (n+1)`.

annotations.label: Fibonacci recurrence relation

anchor.snippet: The Fibonacci numbers may be defined by the recurrence relation

annotations.note: `Nat.fib_add_two : fib (n + 2) = fib n + fib (n + 1)` states the recurrence exactly.

anchors.snippet: The Fibonacci numbers may be defined by the recurrence relation

annotations.label: Negative-index extension

anchor.snippet: The Fibonacci sequence can be extended to negative integer indices by following the same recurrence relation in the negative direction

annotations.note: `Int.fib` extends `Nat.fib` to all integers and `Int.fib_add_two` verifies the recurrence continues to hold.

anchors.snippet: The Fibonacci sequence can be extended to negative integer indices by following the same recurrence relation in the negative direction

annotations.label: Sanskrit prosody pattern count

anchor.snippet: the number of patterns of duration m units is F m +1

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl was found counting Sanskrit prosody (or composition into 1s and 2s) patterns by Fibonacci numbers.

annotations.label: Fibonacci's rabbit problem

anchor.snippet: Fibonacci posed the rabbit math problem : how many pairs will there be in one year?

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize Fibonacci's classical rabbit-pair word problem as a stated proposition.

annotations.label: Binet's formula

anchor.snippet: It has become known as Binet's formula

annotations.note: `Real.coe_fib_eq` states `(Nat.fib n : ℝ) = (φ^n - ψ^n) / √5`, i.e. Binet's formula.

annotations.label: Fibonacci by rounding

anchor.snippet: the number F n is the closest integer to

annotations.note: The Binet-rounding identity `fib n = round (φ^n / √5)` is not stated in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: the number F n is the closest integer to

annotations.label: Floor-function index formula

anchor.snippet: Instead using the floor function gives the largest index of a Fibonacci number that is not greater than F

annotations.note: `Nat.greatestFib` returns the greatest index of a Fibonacci number ≤ n, but the floor/log-φ closed form is not established.

anchors.snippet: Instead using the floor function gives the largest index of a Fibonacci number that is not greater than F

annotations.label: Digit count of F_n

anchor.snippet: the number of digits in F n is asymptotic to

annotations.note: Mathlib has no asymptotic statement about the digit length of `Nat.fib n`.

annotations.label: 4 or 5 Fibonacci numbers per digit length

anchor.snippet: for every integer d > 1 there are either 4 or 5 Fibonacci numbers with d decimal digits

annotations.note: No Mathlib statement about the count of Fibonacci numbers per decimal digit length was found.

annotations.label: Convergence of consecutive ratios to golden ratio

anchor.snippet: the ratio of consecutive Fibonacci numbers converges

annotations.note: `tendsto_fib_succ_div_fib_atTop` shows `fib (n+1)/fib n → φ` as `n → ∞`.

anchors.snippet: the ratio of consecutive Fibonacci numbers converges

annotations.label: Decomposition of powers of phi

anchor.snippet: The resulting recurrence relationships yield Fibonacci numbers as the linear coefficients

annotations.note: `goldenRatio_mul_fib_succ_add_fib` proves `φ^(n+1) = φ · fib(n+1) + fib n`, expressing powers of φ as linear combinations with Fibonacci coefficients.

anchors.snippet: The resulting recurrence relationships yield Fibonacci numbers as the linear coefficients

annotations.label: Perfect-square identification of Fibonacci numbers

anchor.snippet: a positive integer x is a Fibonacci number if and only if at least one of

annotations.note: The square-test characterization (`5x² ± 4` is a perfect square) of Fibonacci numbers is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Matrix form of Fibonacci

anchor.snippet: A 2-dimensional system of linear difference equations that describes the Fibonacci sequence

annotations.note: No matrix representation of the Fibonacci recurrence (e.g., `[[1,1],[1,0]]^n`) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cassini's identity (via determinant)

anchor.snippet: Taking the determinant of both sides of this equation yields Cassini's identity

annotations.note: Cassini's identity itself is formalized for `Int.fib`, but the proof via the matrix-power determinant is not given since no Fibonacci matrix form exists.

anchors.snippet: Taking the determinant of both sides of this equation yields Cassini's identity

annotations.label: Combinatorial interpretation of F_n

anchor.snippet: can be interpreted as the number of (possibly empty) sequences of 1s and 2s whose sum is

annotations.note: No combinatorial interpretation of `fib n` as compositions of `n-1` into 1s and 2s appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sum of first n Fibonacci numbers

anchor.snippet: the sum of the first Fibonacci numbers up to the n -th is equal to the ( n + 2) -th Fibonacci number minus 1

annotations.note: `Nat.fib_succ_eq_succ_sum` proves `fib (n+1) = (∑ k ∈ range n, fib k) + 1`.

anchors.snippet: the sum of the first Fibonacci numbers up to the n -th is equal to the ( n + 2) -th Fibonacci number minus 1

annotations.label: Sums by odd/even index

anchor.snippet: the sum of the first Fibonacci numbers with odd index up to

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state the partial sums of Fibonacci numbers restricted to odd or even indices.

anchors.snippet: the sum of the first Fibonacci numbers with odd index up to

annotations.label: Sum of squares identity

anchor.snippet: the sum of the squares of the first Fibonacci numbers up to

annotations.note: The identity `∑ k≤n, fib k ^ 2 = fib n · fib (n+1)` is not present in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: the sum of the squares of the first Fibonacci numbers up to

anchor.snippet: Cassini's identity states that

annotations.note: `Int.fib_succ_mul_fib_pred_sub_fib_sq` proves `fib (n+1) · fib (n-1) - fib n ^ 2 = (-1)^|n|`.

anchors.snippet: Cassini's identity states that

annotations.label: Catalan's identity

anchor.snippet: Catalan's identity is a generalization

annotations.note: `Int.fib_add_sq_sub_fib_mul_fib_add_two_mul` states Catalan's identity for integer indices.

anchors.snippet: Catalan's identity is a generalization

annotations.label: d'Ocagne's identity

anchor.snippet: where L n is the n -th Lucas number

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Lucas numbers and does not state d'Ocagne's identity.

anchors.snippet: where L n is the n -th Lucas number

annotations.label: Ordinary generating function

anchor.snippet: The ordinary generating function of the Fibonacci sequence is the power series

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state the ordinary generating function `∑ fib n · x^n = x/(1-x-x²)`.

annotations.label: Exponential generating function

anchor.snippet: The exponential generating function of the Fibonacci sequence may also be derived from the recurrence relation

annotations.note: Mathlib does not give the exponential generating function of the Fibonacci sequence.

anchors.snippet: The exponential generating function of the Fibonacci sequence may also be derived from the recurrence relation

annotations.label: Sum of odd-indexed reciprocals

anchor.snippet: the sum of every odd-indexed reciprocal Fibonacci number can be written as

annotations.note: No formalization of the closed form for the sum of odd-indexed reciprocal Fibonacci numbers is in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: the sum of every odd-indexed reciprocal Fibonacci number can be written as

annotations.label: Sum of squared reciprocals

anchor.snippet: the sum of squared reciprocal Fibonacci numbers as

annotations.note: The closed form for `∑ 1/fib n^2` is not present in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: the sum of squared reciprocal Fibonacci numbers as

annotations.label: Sum of even-indexed reciprocals

anchor.snippet: The sum of all even-indexed reciprocal Fibonacci numbers is

annotations.note: No formula for `∑ 1/fib (2n)` is in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: The sum of all even-indexed reciprocal Fibonacci numbers is

annotations.label: Irrationality of reciprocal Fibonacci constant

anchor.snippet: this number has been proved irrational by Richard André-Jeannin

annotations.note: André-Jeannin's irrationality of `∑ 1/fib n` is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Millin's series

anchor.snippet: Millin's series gives the identity

annotations.note: Millin's series identity for `∑ 1/fib (2^n)` is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Millin's series gives the identity

annotations.label: GCD divisibility property

anchor.snippet: the Fibonacci sequence satisfies the stronger divisibility property

annotations.note: `Nat.fib_gcd` proves `fib (gcd m n) = gcd (fib m) (fib n)`, the strong divisibility property.

anchors.snippet: the Fibonacci sequence satisfies the stronger divisibility property

annotations.label: Consecutive Fibonacci numbers are coprime

anchor.snippet: any three consecutive Fibonacci numbers are pairwise coprime

annotations.note: `Nat.fib_coprime_fib_succ` establishes that two consecutive Fibonacci numbers are coprime; the explicit pairwise coprimality of any three consecutive is not stated.

anchors.snippet: any three consecutive Fibonacci numbers are pairwise coprime

annotations.label: Prime divisibility by Legendre symbol mod 5

anchor.snippet: Every prime number p divides a Fibonacci number that can be determined by the value of p modulo 5

annotations.note: The rank-of-apparition theorem expressed via the Legendre symbol mod 5 is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fibonacci pseudoprime

anchor.snippet: If n is composite and satisfies the formula, then n is a Fibonacci pseudoprime

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of Fibonacci pseudoprime.

anchors.snippet: If n is composite and satisfies the formula, then n is a Fibonacci pseudoprime

annotations.label: Fibonacci prime

anchor.snippet: A Fibonacci prime is a Fibonacci number that is prime

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define `Fibonacci prime` as a named notion.

annotations.label: Prime-index requirement for Fibonacci primes

anchor.snippet: any Fibonacci prime must have a prime index

annotations.note: `Nat.fib_dvd` gives `m ∣ n → fib m ∣ fib n`, from which prime-index-of-prime-fib follows, but the conclusion itself is not stated.

annotations.label: Fibonacci near a prime bound

anchor.snippet: No Fibonacci number greater than F 6 = 8 is one greater or one less than a prime number

annotations.note: No Mathlib statement about Fibonacci numbers being one off from primes was found.

annotations.label: Only nontrivial square Fibonacci is 144

anchor.snippet: The only nontrivial square Fibonacci number is 144

annotations.note: Cohn's theorem that 144 is the unique nontrivial square Fibonacci number is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finitely many perfect-power Fibonacci numbers

anchor.snippet: Attila Pethő proved in 2001 that there is only a finite number of perfect power Fibonacci numbers

annotations.note: Pethő's finiteness result for perfect-power Fibonacci numbers is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Only perfect-power Fibonacci numbers are 8 and 144

anchor.snippet: Bugeaud, M. Mignotte, and S. Siksek proved that 8 and 144 are the only such non-trivial perfect powers

annotations.note: The Bugeaud–Mignotte–Siksek theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Triangular Fibonacci numbers

anchor.snippet: The only triangular Fibonacci numbers are 1, 3, 21, and 55

annotations.note: The classification of triangular Fibonacci numbers is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No Fibonacci is perfect

anchor.snippet: No Fibonacci number can be a perfect number

annotations.note: No Mathlib statement proves that no Fibonacci number is a perfect number.

annotations.label: Carmichael's theorem for Fibonacci

anchor.snippet: every Fibonacci number has a prime factor that is not a factor of any smaller Fibonacci number

annotations.note: Carmichael's primitive-divisor theorem for Fibonacci numbers is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wall–Sun–Sun prime

anchor.snippet: Such primes (if there are any) would be called Wall–Sun–Sun primes

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define Wall–Sun–Sun primes.

annotations.label: Odd prime divisors of F_n for odd n

anchor.snippet: all odd prime divisors of F n are congruent to 1 modulo 4

annotations.note: No statement about the mod-4 residue of odd prime divisors of `fib n` is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pisano period

anchor.snippet: the resulting sequence is periodic with period at most 6 n

annotations.note: No definition of the Pisano period of Fibonacci modulo `n` was found.

annotations.label: Fibonacci in Pascal's triangle

anchor.snippet: The Fibonacci numbers occur as the sums of binomial coefficients in the "shallow" diagonals of Pascal's triangle

annotations.note: `Nat.fib_succ_eq_sum_choose` writes `fib (n+1)` as a sum of binomial coefficients along the antidiagonal — the shallow-diagonal Pascal identity.

anchors.snippet: The Fibonacci numbers occur as the sums of binomial coefficients in the "shallow" diagonals of Pascal's triangle

annotations.label: Compositions and domino tilings count

anchor.snippet: counting the number of ways of writing a given number n as an ordered sum of 1s and 2s

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state the bijection between compositions/domino tilings and Fibonacci numbers.

annotations.label: Binary strings without consecutive 1s

anchor.snippet: The number of binary strings of length n without consecutive 1 s is the Fibonacci number F n +2

annotations.note: No Mathlib enumeration of binary strings without consecutive 1s by `fib (n+2)` was found.

annotations.label: Binary strings without odd-run of 1s

anchor.snippet: The number of binary strings of length n without an odd number of consecutive 1 s is the Fibonacci number F n +1

annotations.note: Not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Binary strings without even-runs

anchor.snippet: The number of binary strings of length n without an even number of consecutive 0 s or 1 s is 2 F n

annotations.note: Not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diophantine definition of Fibonacci

anchor.snippet: the Fibonacci numbers can be defined by a Diophantine equation

annotations.note: The Diophantine characterization of Fibonacci numbers (e.g., Jones's polynomial) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fibonacci is a complete sequence

anchor.snippet: The Fibonacci numbers are also an example of a complete sequence

annotations.note: Zeckendorf's representation (`Nat.zeckendorfEquiv`) implicitly entails completeness of the Fibonacci sequence, but no separate `IsComplete`-style decl is stated.

annotations.label: Zeckendorf's theorem

anchor.snippet: This is known as Zeckendorf's theorem

annotations.note: `Nat.zeckendorfEquiv` states Zeckendorf's theorem as an equivalence between naturals and Zeckendorf representations.

annotations.label: Pythagorean triples from Fibonacci

anchor.snippet: every second Fibonacci number is the length of the hypotenuse of a right triangle with integer sides

annotations.note: No construction of Pythagorean triples from Fibonacci numbers is in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: every second Fibonacci number is the length of the hypotenuse of a right triangle with integer sides

annotations.label: Ring lemma involvement

anchor.snippet: Fibonacci numbers appear in the ring lemma

annotations.note: The ring lemma of disk-packing geometry is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Worst case of Euclid's algorithm

anchor.snippet: the worst case input for this algorithm is a pair of consecutive Fibonacci numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib bounds continued-fraction denominators below by `Nat.fib`, which underlies the worst-case analysis, but does not explicitly state the Lamé worst-case theorem for the Euclidean algorithm.

annotations.label: Fibonacci tree

anchor.snippet: A Fibonacci tree is a binary tree whose child trees (recursively) differ in height by exactly 1

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define Fibonacci trees.

annotations.label: Vogel sunflower floret model

anchor.snippet: A model for the pattern of florets in the head of a sunflower was proposed by Helmut Vogel

annotations.note: Vogel's sunflower model is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bee ancestry Fibonacci

anchor.snippet: Fibonacci numbers also appear in the ancestral pedigrees of bees

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: X chromosome ancestry

anchor.snippet: the number of possible ancestors on the human X chromosome inheritance line at a given ancestral generation also follows the Fibonacci sequence

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Light reflection paths in plates

anchor.snippet: The number of different beam paths that have k reflections, for k > 1 , is the k -th Fibonacci number

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Miles-to-kilometers conversion

anchor.snippet: the decomposition of distance in miles into a sum of Fibonacci numbers becomes nearly the kilometer sum

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Infinite resistor chain

anchor.snippet: The measured values of voltages and currents in the infinite resistor chain circuit

annotations.note: Not formalized.
